name: Edge Finishing
description: The process of transforming a raw cut leather edge into a smooth, sealed finish. Involves beveling, dampening, and burnishing.
steps: Run an edge beveler (#1 or #2) along both faces of each raw edge.
Dampen the edge with a fingertip dipped in water.
Rub the edge firmly with a bone folder or wooden slicker in short strokes.
Repeat dampening and burnishing until the edge compresses to a smooth finish.
Optional: apply edge paint for a sealed, coloured finish.
